content.text:
**Verification PASSED: 20/20 non-degenerate cases are bit-identical** to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` — both `replace` modes, both orientations, multiple depths, seeded and unseeded, and subsets.

The degenerate `sample_size=1` case diverges as expected, but my annotation there is self-contradictory (phyloseq's sums are still 1; the bug is in *which* OTU gets the read, not the sum). Let me fix that block to demonstrate the actual bug cleanly:

input.old_string:
cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("Here phyloseq's sample() edge-case bug makes the two DIVERGE by design.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)
# Show MiscMetabar is the CORRECT one: every retained sample sums to exactly 1.
b_deg <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE)
cat("MiscMetabar: all retained sample sums == 1:",
  all(sample_sums(b_deg) == 1), "\n")
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm, sample.size = 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
cat("phyloseq:    all retained sample sums == 1:",
  all(sample_sums(a_deg) == 1), "  (FALSE => phyloseq bug)\n")
input.new_string:
cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("A single-read sample must keep its one read in its ORIGINAL OTU. phyloseq's\n")
cat("`sample(x, 1)` scalar gotcha instead scatters it to a random OTU.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)

# Focus on samples that hold exactly one read: which OTU ends up with that read?
otu_canon <- as_canon(dfm)
single <- colnames(otu_canon)[colSums(otu_canon) == 1]
true_otu <- apply(otu_canon[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

b_deg <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE)
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm, sample.size = 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
b_otu <- apply(as_canon(b_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)
a_otu <- apply(as_canon(a_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

cat(sprintf("Single-read samples: %d\n", length(single)))
cat(sprintf("MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d\n",
  sum(b_otu == true_otu), length(single)))
cat(sprintf("phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d  (< %d => phyloseq bug)\n",
  sum(a_otu == true_otu), length(single), length(single)))

# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`.rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
#
# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
# random index, not `x`) that `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
#
# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R

.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))

cat("R:", R.version.string, "| phyloseq:", as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")), "\n\n")

# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) set.seed(1)

# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
as_canon <- function(ps) {
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  m
}

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace)
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace)
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label, replace = replace, sample_size = ss, seed = as.character(seed),
    n_samp_phyloseq = nsamples(a), n_samp_pq = nsamples(b),
    same_samples = same_samples, same_dims = same_dims,
    identical = identical_counts, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

dfm <- data_fungi_mini
dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths

grid <- expand.grid(
  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
  seed = c(1, 123),

)

cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")

for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  g <- grid[i, ]
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_rows")
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_col, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_cols")
}
# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, 800, FALSE, 7, "subset_norepl")

tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))

cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("Here phyloseq's sample() edge-case bug makes the two DIVERGE by design.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab)
cat(if (ok) {
  "PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all\nnon-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.\n"

      TRUE     FALSE
Single-read samples: 14
MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: 14 / 14
phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: 7 / 14  (< 14 => phyloseq bug)

================ VERDICT ================
PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all
non-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.

content.text:
The verification is now unambiguous:
- **20/20 non-degenerate cases bit-identical** to phyloseq.
- Degenerate case: **MiscMetabar keeps the single read in its original OTU 14/14; phyloseq only 7/14** (the `sample()` scalar gotcha scatters it). So the custom function is equivalent *and* strictly more correct.

This proves the internal swaps are behavior-preserving. Before I edit the ~13 call sites across 6 files, let me get the advisor's read on the refactor plan.

Two gaps in the equivalence proof block the 12-site swap as-is. Both trace to the same root: your `compare()` harness tests the helper against a phyloseq configuration that doesn't match how those 12 sites actually call phyloseq. The 20/20 bit-identity is real and solid — don't redo it — but it doesn't cover the substitution you're about to make.

**1. `trimOTUs` — blocking, concrete.** Your `compare()` forces `trimOTUs = FALSE` on phyloseq to match the helper. But the 12 internal sites call `rarefy_even_depth(physeq, rngseed=…)` with **no** `trimOTUs` arg → they get phyloseq's default **`trimOTUs = TRUE`**, which prunes taxa that go all-zero after subsampling. Your helper never trims. So your proof validates a config the call sites don't use; the actual swap changes `ntaxa` (and everything downstream of it) at those 12 sites. (Your *original* `rarefy_pq` call used `trimOTUs=FALSE`, so the helper is correct for `rarefy_pq` itself — it's only the borrowed sites that differ.)

Fix: add a `trimOTUs` arg to the helper (post-step `prune_taxa(taxa_sums(x) > 0, x)`), default `TRUE` to match phyloseq; have `rarefy_pq` pass `FALSE`. Keep `rarefy_pq` at `FALSE` deliberately — `n>1` averaging via `Reduce("+")` needs all reps to share dims, which trimming would break. Then extend the verification grid to `trimOTUs=TRUE`.

**2. RNG-state restoration — verify before swapping.** Your earlier error (`object '.Random.seed' not found` at phyloseq entry, *with* `rngseed=number`) is the tell: phyloseq reads `.Random.seed` on entry, which means it almost certainly saves and **restores** it on exit. Your helper sets the seed and leaves the RNG advanced. Table output is identical (proven), but the *post-call RNG state* differs — and `beta_div_test.R` rarefies and then runs `adonis2` (a permutation test) off the global RNG. After the swap, those permutation p-values would change.

Discriminating test:
```r
set.seed(42); s1 <- .Random.seed
invisible(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(dfm, sample.size=500, rngseed=7, trimOTUs=FALSE, verbose=FALSE))
identical(s1, .Random.seed)        # TRUE => phyloseq restores
set.seed(42); s1 <- .Random.seed
invisible(.rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 500, rngseed=7))
identical(s1, .Random.seed)        # compare
```
If phyloseq restores and the helper doesn't: either `on.exit()`-restore `.Random.seed` in the helper (true drop-in; compatible with the `n>1` loop since each rep seeds explicitly), or run the rarefy-enabled `adonis_pq`/beta_div_test path old-vs-new and confirm identical output end-to-end.

Minor, non-blocking: the 12 sites pass `verbose=verbose`; the helper drops it, so the "N samples removed" messages disappear. Acceptable under silent-by-default, just know it's a behavior change.

**Scope check, worth saying to the user:** only the `replace=FALSE` path was the CRAN bug, and that's `rarefy_pq` — already fixed and verified. The 12 `replace=TRUE` sites are **not** broken on R-devel. This refactor is optional consistency work on currently-working code; it adds swap risk to a resubmission you're under archival time pressure to land. Reasonable to want, but consider shipping the done-and-verified `rarefy_pq` fix for CRAN now and doing the broad swap as a separate follow-up.

#' Only the \code{otu_table} component is modified.
#'
#' @seealso
#' \code{\link{sample}}
#' 
#' \code{\link{set.seed}}
#'
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
#' # Test with esophagus dataset
#' data("esophagus")
#' esorepT = rarefy_even_depth(esophagus, replace=TRUE)
#' esorepF = rarefy_even_depth(esophagus, replace=FALSE)
#' sample_sums(esophagus)
#' sample_sums(esorepT)
#' sample_sums(esorepF)
#' ## NRun Manually: Too slow!
#' # data("GlobalPatterns")
#' # GPrepT = rarefy_even_depth(GlobalPatterns, 1E5, replace=TRUE)
#' ## Actually just this one is slow
#' # system.time(GPrepF <- rarefy_even_depth(GlobalPatterns, 1E5, replace=FALSE))
rarefy_even_depth <- function(physeq, sample.size=min(sample_sums(physeq)),
															rngseed=FALSE, replace=TRUE, trimOTUs=TRUE, verbose=TRUE){
	
	if( as(rngseed, "logical") ){
		# Save current RNG state, set seed, restore on exit
		old_seed <- .Random.seed
		on.exit(assign(".Random.seed", old_seed, envir = .GlobalEnv), add = TRUE)
		set.seed(rngseed)
    if(verbose){
      # Print to screen this value
      message("`set.seed(", rngseed, ")` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.")
      message("Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.")
      message("Try `set.seed(", rngseed,"); .Random.seed` for the full vector", sep="")
      message("...")
    }
	} else if(verbose){
		message("You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded\n",
				" the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n",
				"See `?set.seed`\n")

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace)
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace)
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label, replace = replace, sample_size = ss, seed = as.character(seed),
    n_samp_phyloseq = nsamples(a), n_samp_pq = nsamples(b),
    same_samples = same_samples, same_dims = same_dims,
    identical = identical_counts, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`.rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
#
# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
# random index, not `x`) that `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
#
# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R

.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))

cat("R:", R.version.string, "| phyloseq:", as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")), "\n\n")

# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) set.seed(1)

# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
as_canon <- function(ps) {
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  m
}

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace)
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace)
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label, replace = replace, sample_size = ss, seed = as.character(seed),
    n_samp_phyloseq = nsamples(a), n_samp_pq = nsamples(b),
    same_samples = same_samples, same_dims = same_dims,
    identical = identical_counts, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

dfm <- data_fungi_mini
dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths

grid <- expand.grid(
  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
  seed = c(1, 123),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")
res <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  g <- grid[i, ]
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_rows")
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_col, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_cols")
}
# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, 800, FALSE, 7, "subset_norepl")

tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))

cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("A single-read sample must keep its one read in its ORIGINAL OTU. phyloseq's\n")
cat("`sample(x, 1)` scalar gotcha instead scatters it to a random OTU.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)

# Focus on samples that hold exactly one read: which OTU ends up with that read?
otu_canon <- as_canon(dfm)
single <- colnames(otu_canon)[colSums(otu_canon) == 1]
true_otu <- apply(otu_canon[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

b_deg <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE)
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm, sample.size = 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
b_otu <- apply(as_canon(b_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)
a_otu <- apply(as_canon(a_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

cat(sprintf("Single-read samples: %d\n", length(single)))
cat(sprintf("MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d\n",
  sum(b_otu == true_otu), length(single)))
cat(sprintf("phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d  (< %d => phyloseq bug)\n",
  sum(a_otu == true_otu), length(single), length(single)))

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab)
cat(if (ok) {
  "PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all\nnon-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.\n"
} else {
  "FAIL: divergence found in a non-degenerate case (see `identical = FALSE`).\n"
})
quit(status = if (ok) 0 else 1)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`.rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
#
# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
# random index, not `x`) that `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
#
# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R

.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))

cat("R:", R.version.string, "| phyloseq:", as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")), "\n\n")

# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) set.seed(1)

# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
as_canon <- function(ps) {
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  m
}

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label, trimOTUs = FALSE) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs, verbose = FALSE))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs)
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs, verbose = FALSE))
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs)
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_taxa <- setequal(taxa_names(a), taxa_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label, replace = replace, sample_size = ss, seed = as.character(seed),
    trim = trimOTUs, ntaxa_phylo = ntaxa(a), ntaxa_pq = ntaxa(b),
    same_samples = same_samples, same_taxa = same_taxa,
    same_dims = same_dims, identical = identical_counts,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

dfm <- data_fungi_mini
dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths

grid <- expand.grid(
  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
  seed = c(1, 123),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")
res <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  g <- grid[i, ]
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_rows")
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_col, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_cols")
}
# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, 800, FALSE, 7, "subset_norepl")

tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))

cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("A single-read sample must keep its one read in its ORIGINAL OTU. phyloseq's\n")
cat("`sample(x, 1)` scalar gotcha instead scatters it to a random OTU.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)

# Focus on samples that hold exactly one read: which OTU ends up with that read?
otu_canon <- as_canon(dfm)
single <- colnames(otu_canon)[colSums(otu_canon) == 1]
true_otu <- apply(otu_canon[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

b_deg <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE)
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm, sample.size = 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
b_otu <- apply(as_canon(b_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)
a_otu <- apply(as_canon(a_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

cat(sprintf("Single-read samples: %d\n", length(single)))
cat(sprintf("MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d\n",
  sum(b_otu == true_otu), length(single)))
cat(sprintf("phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d  (< %d => phyloseq bug)\n",
  sum(a_otu == true_otu), length(single), length(single)))

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab)
cat(if (ok) {
  "PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all\nnon-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.\n"
} else {
  "FAIL: divergence found in a non-degenerate case (see `identical = FALSE`).\n"
})
quit(status = if (ok) 0 else 1)

content.text: Now add an RNG-state restoration test and fold it into the verdict:

input.new_string:
cat("\n=== RNG-state restoration (phyloseq saves/restores .Random.seed) ===\n")
rng_ok <- TRUE
for (rep in c(TRUE, FALSE)) {
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
    dfm, sample.size = 500, rngseed = 7, replace = rep,
    trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)))
  phylo_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(.rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 500, rngseed = 7, replace = rep))
  pq_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  match <- phylo_restores == pq_restores
  rng_ok <- rng_ok && match
  cat(sprintf("replace=%-5s | phyloseq restores: %s | pq restores: %s | match: %s\n",
    rep, phylo_restores, pq_restores, match))
}

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab) && rng_ok

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`.rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
#
# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
# random index, not `x`) that `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
#
# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R

.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))

cat("R:", R.version.string, "| phyloseq:", as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")), "\n\n")

# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) set.seed(1)

# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
as_canon <- function(ps) {
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) m <- t(m)
  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  m
}

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label, trimOTUs = FALSE) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs, verbose = FALSE))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = FALSE, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs)
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps, sample.size = ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs, verbose = FALSE))
    b <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(ps, ss, rngseed = seed, replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs)
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_taxa <- setequal(taxa_names(a), taxa_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label, replace = replace, sample_size = ss, seed = as.character(seed),
    trim = trimOTUs, ntaxa_phylo = ntaxa(a), ntaxa_pq = ntaxa(b),
    same_samples = same_samples, same_taxa = same_taxa,
    same_dims = same_dims, identical = identical_counts,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

dfm <- data_fungi_mini
dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths

grid <- expand.grid(
  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
  seed = c(1, 123),
  trimOTUs = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")
res <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  g <- grid[i, ]
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_rows", trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs)
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_col, g$sample_size, g$replace, g$seed,
    "taxa_cols", trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs)
}
# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage (incl. heavy trimming at 1000)
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, 800, FALSE, 7, "subset_norepl",
  trimOTUs = TRUE)

tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))

cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat("A single-read sample must keep its one read in its ORIGINAL OTU. phyloseq's\n")
cat("`sample(x, 1)` scalar gotcha instead scatters it to a random OTU.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)

# Focus on samples that hold exactly one read: which OTU ends up with that read?
otu_canon <- as_canon(dfm)
single <- colnames(otu_canon)[colSums(otu_canon) == 1]
true_otu <- apply(otu_canon[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

b_deg <- .rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE)
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm, sample.size = 1, rngseed = 1, replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE, verbose = FALSE))
b_otu <- apply(as_canon(b_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)
a_otu <- apply(as_canon(a_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

cat(sprintf("Single-read samples: %d\n", length(single)))
cat(sprintf("MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d\n",
  sum(b_otu == true_otu), length(single)))
cat(sprintf("phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d  (< %d => phyloseq bug)\n",
  sum(a_otu == true_otu), length(single), length(single)))

cat("\n=== RNG-state restoration (phyloseq saves/restores .Random.seed) ===\n")
rng_ok <- TRUE
for (rep in c(TRUE, FALSE)) {
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
    dfm, sample.size = 500, rngseed = 7, replace = rep,
    trimOTUs = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)))
  phylo_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(.rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 500, rngseed = 7, replace = rep))
  pq_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  match <- phylo_restores == pq_restores
  rng_ok <- rng_ok && match
  cat(sprintf("replace=%-5s | phyloseq restores: %s | pq restores: %s | match: %s\n",
    rep, phylo_restores, pq_restores, match))
}

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab) && rng_ok
cat(if (ok) {
  "PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all\nnon-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.\n"
} else {
  "FAIL: divergence found in a non-degenerate case (see `identical = FALSE`).\n"
})
quit(status = if (ok) 0 else 1)

toolUseResult.originalFile: ################################################################################ #' Hill diversity profile for a phyloseq object #' #' @description #' #' #' #' Wraps [divent::profile_hill()] to compute a Hill diversity profile across #' diversity orders for each sample in a `phyloseq` object, and returns a #' ggplot2 object via [ggplot2::autoplot()]. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param orders (numeric vector) Hill diversity orders to compute. Default #' `seq(0, 2, 0.1)`. #' @param merge_sample_by (character or NULL) If not NULL, merge samples #' using [merge_samples2()] before computing profiles. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [divent::profile_hill()]. #' #' @return A ggplot2 object. #' @seealso [divent::profile_hill()], [hill_curves_pq()] #' @importFrom divent profile_hill accum_hill div_hill ent_shannon ent_simpson #' @export #' @examples #' \donttest{ #' profile_hill_pq( #' prune_samples(sample_names(data_fungi_mini)[1:5], data_fungi_mini), #' orders = c(0, 1, 2) #' ) #' } profile_hill_pq <- function( physeq, orders = seq(0, 2, 0.1), merge_sample_by = NULL, ... ) { verify_pq(physeq) if (!is.null(merge_sample_by)) { physeq <- merge_samples2(physeq, merge_sample_by) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq)) sample_names <- rownames(otu) results <- lapply( cli::cli_progress_along(sample_names, name = "Computing Hill profiles"), \(i) { abund <- as.numeric(otu[i, ]) abund <- abund[abund > 0] res <- divent::profile_hill(abund, orders = orders, ...) res$site <- sample_names[i] res } ) combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(results) class(combined) <- c("profile", class(combined)) ggplot2::autoplot(combined) } ################################################################################ #' Hill diversity accumulation curve for a phyloseq object (default: q = 1) #' #' @description #' #' #' #' Computes Hill diversity accumulation curves from a `phyloseq` object #' and returns a ggplot2 object. #' #' Two types of curves are available: #' - `type = "individual"` (default): individual-based (sequence-based) #' rarefaction/extrapolation curves via [divent::accum_hill()], with one #' curve per sample (or per merged group). #' - `type = "sample"`: sample-based accumulation curve. Samples are pooled #' incrementally (over random permutations) and Hill diversity is computed #' at each step using [divent::div_hill()]. The x-axis represents the #' number of samples. #' #' @inheritParams clean_pq #' @param q (numeric, default 1) Hill diversity order. Default is 1 (exponential #' of Shannon entropy), recommended for its robustness against rare and #' potentially erroneous sequences (Alberdi & Gilbert, 2019; #' Calderón-Sanou et al., 2019). #' @param type (character) Type of accumulation curve. Either `"individual"` #' (sequence-based, one curve per sample) or `"sample"` (sample-based, #' one curve for the whole dataset or per group). #' @param merge_sample_by (character or NULL) Variable name in `sam_data` used #' to group samples. Behaviour differs by `type`: #' - `type = "individual"`: samples are merged before computing curves #' (one curve per merged group, using [merge_samples2()]). #' - `type = "sample"`: samples are split by group and one accumulation #' curve is drawn per group, all on the same plot. #' @param n_permutations (integer, default 100) Number of random sample #' orderings used to compute the mean and confidence envelope for #' sample-based accumulation. Ignored when `type = "individual"`. #' @param conf_level (numeric, default 0.95) Confidence level for the #' envelope around sample-based accumulation curves. Ignored when #' `type = "individual"`. #' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [divent::accum_hill()] (when #' `type = "individual"`) or [divent::div_hill()] (when `type = "sample"`). #' #' @return A ggplot2 object. #' @references #' Alberdi, A., & Gilbert, M. T. P. (2019). A guide to the application of #' Hill numbers to DNA-based diversity analyses. *Molecular Ecology Resources*. #' \doi{10.1111/1755-0998.13014} #' #' Calderón-Sanou, I., Münkemüller, T., Boyer, F., Zinger, L., & Thuiller, W. #' (2019). From environmental DNA sequences to ecological conclusions: How #' strong is the influence of methodological choices? *Journal of Biogeography*, #' 47. \doi{10.1111/jbi.13681} #' @seealso [divent::accum_hill()], [divent::div_hill()], [hill_curves_pq()] #' @export #' @examples #' \dontrun{ #' # Individual (sequence-based) accumulation curves #' hill_acc_pq(rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi_mini, sample.size = 500), #' n_permutations = 3 #' ) + no_legend() #' hill_acc_pq(rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi_mini, sample.size = 500), #' n_permutations = 3, #' merge_sample_by = "Height" #' ) #' #' # Sample-based accumulation curve #' hill_acc_pq(data_fungi_mini, type = "sample", n_permutations = 3) #' hill_acc_pq(data_fungi_mini, #' type = "sample", merge_sample_by = "Height", #' n_permutations = 3 #' ) #' } hill_acc_pq <- function( physeq, q = 1, type = c("individual", "sample"), merge_sample_by = NULL, n_permutations = 100, conf_level = 0.95, ... ) { type <- match.arg(type) verify_pq(physeq) if (type == "individual") { if (!is.null(merge_sample_by)) { physeq <- merge_samples2(physeq, merge_sample_by) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq)) return(.hill_acc_individual(otu, q = q, ...)) } physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq)) if (is.null(merge_sample_by)) { return(.hill_acc_sample( otu, q = q, n_permutations = n_permutations, conf_level = conf_level, ... )) } # Split samples by group and draw one curve per group groups <- as.character(phyloseq::sample_data(physeq)[[merge_sample_by]]) group_levels <- unique(groups[!is.na(groups)]) results <- lapply(group_levels, function(g) { idx <- which(groups == g) accum <- .hill_acc_sample( otu[idx, , drop = FALSE], q = q, n_permutations = n_permutations, conf_level = conf_level, ..., .return_df = TRUE ) accum$group <- g accum }) combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(results) ggplot2::ggplot( combined, ggplot2::aes( x = .data$level, y = .data$diversity, color = .data$group, fill = .data$group ) ) + ggplot2::geom_ribbon( ggplot2::aes(ymin = .data$lwr, ymax = .data$upr), alpha = 0.2, color = NA ) + ggplot2::geom_line() + ggplot2::geom_point() + ggplot2::labs( title = paste("Sample-based Hill accumulation curve (q =", q, ")"), x = "Number of samples", y = paste("Hill diversity (q =", q, ")"), color = merge_sample_by, fill = merge_sample_by ) } #' @noRd .hill_acc_individual <- function(otu, q, ...) { sample_names <- rownames(otu) results <- lapply( cli::cli_progress_along( sample_names, name = "Computing individual-based accumulation curves" ), \(i) { abund <- as.numeric(otu[i, ]) abund <- abund[abund > 0] res <- divent::accum_hill(abund, q = q, ...) res$site <- sample_names[i] res } ) combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(results) class(combined) <- c("accumulation", class(combined)) ggplot2::autoplot(combined) + ggplot2::labs( title = paste("Hill diversity accumulation curves (q =", q, ")"), x = "Number of sequences", y = paste("Hill diversity (q =", q, ")") ) } #' @noRd .hill_acc_sample <- function( otu, q, n_permutations, conf_level, ..., .return_df = FALSE ) { n_samples <- nrow(otu) otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu) alpha <- 1 - conf_level div_matrix <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n_permutations, ncol = n_samples) for (p in cli::cli_progress_along( seq_len(n_permutations), name = "Computing sample-based accumulation curves" )) { perm <- sample.int(n_samples) pooled_mat <- apply(otu_mat[perm, , drop = FALSE], 2, cumsum) if (n_samples == 1L) { pooled_mat <- matrix(pooled_mat, nrow = 1L) } for (k in seq_len(n_samples)) { pooled <- pooled_mat[k, ] pooled <- pooled[pooled > 0] div_matrix[p, k] <- divent::div_hill( pooled, q = q, as_numeric = TRUE, estimator = "naive", ... ) } } accum_df <- data.frame( level = seq_len(n_samples), diversity = colMeans(div_matrix), lwr = apply(div_matrix, 2, stats::quantile, probs = alpha / 2), upr = apply(div_matrix, 2, stats::quantile, probs = 1 - alpha / 2) ) if (.return_df) { return(accum_df) } ggplot2::ggplot( accum_df, ggplot2::aes(x = .data$level, y = .data$diversity) ) + ggplot2::geom_ribbon( ggplot2::aes(ymin = .data$lwr, ymax = .data$upr), alpha = 0.2 ) + ggplot2::geom_line() + ggplot2::geom_point() + ggplot2::labs( title = paste("Sample-based Hill accumulation curve (q =", q, ")"), x = "Number of samples", y = paste("Hill diversity (q =", q, ")") ) }

Also I notice a phyloseq `set.seed(123)` message — let me confirm that's from a test calling phyloseq directly, not leftover package code. Let me re-run capturing only summaries, and check tests/ for direct phyloseq calls:

input.command:
cd /home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/pqverse/pqverse_pkg/MiscMetabar
echo "=== direct rarefy_even_depth calls in tests/ (ok if present) ==="

Rscript -e '
.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
Sys.setenv(NOT_CRAN="true")
suppressMessages({library(dada2); library(devtools); load_all(".", quiet=TRUE); library(testthat)})
for (f in c("test_beta_div.R","test_alpha_div.R","test_plot_funcs.R")) {
  r <- tryCatch(as.data.frame(test_file(file.path("tests/testthat", f), reporter="silent")),
                error=function(e){cat(f,"ERROR:",conditionMessage(e),"\n");NULL})
  if(!is.null(r)){
    cat(sprintf(">>> %-20s PASS:%d FAIL:%d WARN:%d SKIP:%d\n", f, sum(r$passed), sum(r$failed), sum(r$warning), sum(r$skipped)))
    bad <- r[r$failed>0|r$error,]; if(nrow(bad)) for(i in seq_len(nrow(bad))) cat("    FAIL:", bad$test[i], "\n")
  }
}

data(data_fungi)
data("enterotype")

test_that("dist_bycol works fine", {
  expect_length(
    dist_bycol(
      data_fungi@otu_table,
      as_binary_otu_table(data_fungi)@otu_table
    ),
    2
  )
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_error(length(dist_bycol(
    data_fungi@otu_table,
    enterotype@otu_table
  )))
})

test_that("all_object_size works fine", {
  expect_type(all_object_size(), "double")
})

test_that("diff_fct_diff_class works fine", {
  expect_equal(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      data_fungi@sam_data$Sample_id,
      numeric_fonction = sum,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    17852
  )
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_equal(
    round(
      diff_fct_diff_class(
        data_fungi@sam_data$Time,
        numeric_fonction = mean,
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
      2
    ),
    5.80
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      data_fungi@sam_data$Height == "Low",
      logical_method = "TRUE_if_one"
    ),
    TRUE
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      data_fungi@sam_data$Height == "Low",
      logical_method = "NA_if_not_all_TRUE"
    ),
    NA
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      data_fungi@sam_data$Height == "Low",
      logical_method = "FALSE_if_not_all_TRUE"
    ),
    FALSE
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      data_fungi@sam_data$Height,
      character_method = "unique_or_na"
    ),
    NA_character_
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      c("IE", "IE"),
      character_method = "unique_or_na"
    ),
    "IE"
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      c("IE", "IE", "TE", "TE"),
      character_method = "more_frequent"
    ),
    "IE"
  )
  expect_identical(
    diff_fct_diff_class(
      c("IE", "IE", "TE", "TE"),
      character_method = "more_frequent_without_equality"
    ),
    NA_character_
  )
})

test_that("add_funguild_info works fine", {

test_that("are_modality_even_depth works fine", {
  expect_equal(
    are_modality_even_depth(data_fungi, "Time")$statistic[[1]],
    62.143
  )
  expect_equal(
    are_modality_even_depth(rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi), "Time")$p.value,
    1
  )
  expect_silent(are_modality_even_depth(data_fungi, "Height", boxplot = TRUE))
})


test_that("as_binary_otu_table works fine", {
  expect_s4_class(as_binary_otu_table(enterotype), "phyloseq")
  bin_pq <- as_binary_otu_table(data_fungi)
  expect_true(all(bin_pq@otu_table %in% c(0, 1)))
  skip_on_cran()
  bin_pq_5 <- as_binary_otu_table(data_fungi, min_number = 5)
  expect_true(all(bin_pq_5@otu_table %in% c(0, 1)))
  expect_true(sum(bin_pq_5@otu_table) <= sum(bin_pq@otu_table))
  expect_error(as_binary_otu_table("not_a_phyloseq"))
})


test_that("simplify_taxo works fine", {
  d_fm <- data_fungi_mini
  d_fm@tax_table[, "Species"] <- paste0(
    rep(
      c("s__", "s:"),
      ntaxa(d_fm) / 2
    ),
    d_fm@tax_table[, "Species"]
  )

  expect_s4_class(simplify_taxo(d_fm), "phyloseq")
  skip_on_cran()
  simplified <- simplify_taxo(d_fm)
  expect_false(any(grepl("s__", simplified@tax_table[, "Species"])))
  expect_false(any(grepl("s:", simplified@tax_table[, "Species"])))
  expect_s4_class(simplify_taxo(d_fm, remove_NA = TRUE), "phyloseq")
})


test_that("get_file_extension works fine", {
  expect_identical(get_file_extension("test.fasta"), "fasta")
  expect_identical(
    suppressWarnings(get_file_extension("test.fastq.gz")),
    c("fastq", "gz")
  )
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_warning(get_file_extension("test.file.fasta"))
  expect_error(get_file_extension("test_without_extension"))
})


test_that("perc works fine", {
  expect_identical(perc(0.5), 50)
  expect_identical(perc(1, 2), 50)
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_identical(perc(0.567, accuracy = 1), 56.7)
  expect_identical(perc(0.5, add_symbol = TRUE), "50%")
  expect_identical(perc(1, 4, add_symbol = TRUE), "25%")
})


test_that("funky_color works fine", {
  expect_type(funky_color(5), "character")
  expect_length(funky_color(10), 10)
})


test_that("fac2col works fine", {
  test_fac <- factor(c("A", "B", "A", "C"))
  result <- fac2col(test_fac)
  expect_type(result, "character")
  expect_length(result, 4)
  skip_on_cran()
  result_seed <- fac2col(test_fac, seed = 123)
  expect_type(result_seed, "character")
  test_fac_na <- factor(c("A", "B", NA, "C"))
  result_na <- fac2col(test_fac_na)
  expect_identical(result_na[3], "grey")
})


test_that("transp works fine", {
  expect_type(transp("red"), "character")
  expect_type(transp(c("red", "blue")), "character")
  expect_length(transp(c("red", "blue", "green")), 3)
  result <- transp("red", alpha = 0.5)
  expect_true(grepl("#", result))
})

skip_on_cran()
data("GlobalPatterns", package = "phyloseq")
data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq")
library("divent")

GP <- GlobalPatterns
data_fungi_2trees <-
  subset_samples(
    data_fungi,
    data_fungi@sam_data$Tree_name %in% c("A10-005", "AD30-abm-X")
  )
GP_archae <-
  subset_taxa(GlobalPatterns, GlobalPatterns@tax_table[, 1] == "Archaea")
GP_archae <- clean_pq(rarefy_even_depth(subset_samples_pq(
  GP_archae,
  sample_sums(GP_archae) > 1000
)))
data_basidio <- subset_taxa(data_fungi, Phylum == "Basidiomycota")

test_that("hill_pq works with data_fungi dataset", {
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height")))
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(
    data_fungi_mini,
    "Height",
    add_points = TRUE
  )))
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(
    clean_pq(subset_samples_pq(
      data_fungi_mini,
      !is.na(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height)
    )),
    "Height",
    letters = TRUE
  )))
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(
    hill_pq(
      data_fungi_mini,
      "Height",
      add_points = TRUE,
      color_fac = "Time"
    )
  ))
  expect_silent(
    suppressMessages(
      hill_pq(
        data_fungi_mini,
        "Height",
        add_points = TRUE,
        color_fac = "Time",
        one_plot = TRUE,
        correction_for_sample_size = FALSE
      )
    )
  )
  expect_silent(suppressWarnings(suppressMessages(
    hill_pq(
      clean_pq(subset_samples_pq(
        data_fungi_mini,
        !is.na(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height)
      )),
      "Height",
      add_points = TRUE,
      color_fac = "Time",
      one_plot = TRUE,
      correction_for_sample_size = FALSE,
      letters = TRUE
    )
  )))
  expect_length(hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height", add_points = TRUE), 4)
  expect_s3_class(
    hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height", add_points = TRUE)[[1]],
    "ggplot"
  )
})

test_that("hill_pq works with GP dataset", {
  skip_on_cran()
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType")))
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType", add_points = TRUE)))
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType", letters = TRUE)))
  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType", add_points = TRUE)))
  expect_length(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType", add_points = TRUE), 4)
  expect_s3_class(hill_pq(GP, "SampleType", add_points = TRUE)[[1]], "ggplot")
})

})

test_that("hill_bar_pq works with data_fungi_mini dataset", {
  expect_s3_class(
    suppressMessages(hill_bar_pq(data_fungi_mini, Height, q = 0)),
    "ggplot"
  )
  skip_on_cran()
  # multiple q returns a patchwork
  expect_s3_class(
    suppressMessages(hill_bar_pq(data_fungi_mini, Height, q = c(0, 2))),
    "patchwork"
  )
  # letters disabled
  expect_s3_class(
    suppressMessages(
      hill_bar_pq(data_fungi_mini, Height, q = 0, add_letters = FALSE)
    ),
    "ggplot"
  )
  # no global significance -> all "a" (very low threshold)
  expect_s3_class(
    suppressMessages(
      hill_bar_pq(data_fungi_mini, Height, q = 0, p_threshold = 1e-10)
    ),
    "ggplot"
  )
  # custom y labels
  expect_s3_class(
    suppressMessages(
      hill_bar_pq(
        data_fungi_mini,
        Height,
        q = c(0, 2),
        y_labs = c(Hill_0 = "Richness", Hill_2 = "Simpson")
      )
    ),
    "patchwork"
  )
})

  expect_s3_class(
    ggbetween_pq(
      data_fungi_mini,
      fact = "Height"
    )[[1]],
    "ggplot"
  )
})

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8
input.new_string:
# MiscMetabar 0.16.8.9000
* Rarefaction across the package is now performed by an internal R-version-robust reimplementation rather than `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`, whose `replace = FALSE` code path errors with `invalid 'length.out' value` under recent R-devel (phyloseq issue #1753). The reimplementation is bit-identical to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` for the same `seed`, depth and `replace` value (and is more correct in the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read). This affects `rarefy_pq()`, `adonis_pq()`, `adonis_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq()`, `hill_pq()`, `biplot_pq()`, `ggvenn_pq()`, `upset_pq()`, `ggaluv_pq()` and `ggscatt_pq()`.
* `rarefy_pq()` gains a `replace` argument (default `FALSE`, sampling without replacement) and accepts `seed = FALSE` to leave the random number generator untouched, mirroring `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`.

# MiscMetabar 0.16.8

aiTitle: Debug CRAN submission error for MiscMetabar

content.text:
Everything is green and consistent. The broad refactor is complete and verified.

## Done — rarefaction unified across the package

**What changed**
- New internal `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` — an R-version-robust drop-in for `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()` with **matching defaults** (`rngseed=FALSE, replace=TRUE, trimOTUs=TRUE`), matching RNG save/restore, and matching sample-pruning.
- `rarefy_pq()` now exposes `replace` (default `FALSE`) and `seed=FALSE`, and uses the helper.
- **All 15 internal `rarefy_even_depth()` calls** swapped to `.rarefy_even_depth_pq()` (across `alpha_div_test`, `beta_div_test`, `dada_phyloseq`, `plot_functions`); 5 examples + 2 test fixtures → `rarefy_pq()`. **No `rarefy_even_depth()` calls remain except doc-links.**

**Verification (`dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R`, build-ignored)**
- **36/36 non-degenerate cases bit-identical** to phyloseq (replace T/F × depth × seed × `trimOTUs` T/F × both orientations + unseeded + subsets).
- **RNG-state restoration matches** phyloseq for both `replace` modes.
- Degenerate single-read case: **MiscMetabar 14/14 correct vs phyloseq 7/14** (phyloseq's `sample()` scalar gotcha) — so the custom function is equivalent *and* more correct.

**Tests (R 4.6.0):** `normalize` 43 · `dada_phyloseq` 37 · `alpha_div` 16 · `beta_div` 6 · `plot_funcs` 7 · `misc` 45 — **0 failures.** `air format` + `document` + NEWS done; package loads cleanly.

The advisor's two gaps (phyloseq's default `trimOTUs=TRUE`, and RNG restoration) are both handled and verified.

---

**Two things still open:**

1. **4 archived CRAN packages** (`plogr`, `pryr`, `WikidataQueryServiceR`, `WikidataR`) failed to install — they're archived on current CRAN. I can install them from the CRAN archive (`remotes::install_version()` / `pak::pkg_install("cran/<pkg>@<ver>")`). Want me to?

2. **Before CRAN submission:** this swap goes beyond the minimal fix that unblocks CRAN (only `rarefy_pq`'s `replace=FALSE` path was actually broken on R-devel). It's well-verified, but please run a full `/r-check` (`R CMD check --as-cran`) on R 4.6.0 to confirm the whole examples+tests+vignettes pass end-to-end before submitting. Want me to kick that off?

content.text:
Here's the situation and the path:

- Your `/opt/R/devel` is **r90099 (2026-06-03)** and runs once I shim ICU (`LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/tmp/icu72/lib`).
- The blocker is **not** MiscMetabar — it's that phyloseq won't even *load* under R-devel: `rlang.so: undefined symbol: SETLENGTH`. R-devel's pre-installed library has `rlang` (and likely `cli`/`vctrs`) compiled against an **older** R-devel ABI that removed `SETLENGTH`. They must be rebuilt for r90099.

Also note: to reproduce the *bug* you don't actually need the old MiscMetabar — old `rarefy_pq()` just called `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(..., replace = FALSE)`. Once phyloseq loads, one direct call reproduces it.

Let me rebuild the broken compiled deps into the R-devel library and retry loading phyloseq:

input.command:
cd /tmp
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/tmp/icu72/lib /opt/R/devel/bin/Rscript -e '
.libPaths(c("/tmp/rdevel-lib", .libPaths()))
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))
# Rebuild the tidyverse C-layer packages for the current R-devel ABI
install.packages(c("rlang","cli","vctrs","glue","lifecycle"),
                 lib = "/tmp/rdevel-lib", quiet = TRUE, Ncpus = 4)
cat("rlang now from:", find.package("rlang"), "\n")
ok <- tryCatch({suppressMessages(library(phyloseq)); "phyloseq LOADS"},
               error = function(e) paste("FAIL:", conditionMessage(e)))
cat(ok, "\n")
' 2>&1 | tail -6
input.description: Rebuild rlang/cli/vctrs for R-devel, retry phyloseq
